solutions.solution: From figure (2) to (1), upper and middle elements are encircled by the middle and lower elements respectively. The lower element gets enlarged and a clone of the upper element comes inside it.

From fig (2) to (1), the vertical bar rotates by 90° anti-clockwise, while the horizontal bar rotates by 90° clockwise. The upper-left element goes to upper right, upper right to lower left, lower left to lower right, lower right to the upper left.
In all other pairs, the existing symbol at the central position shifts to the middle of the border line and two new identical symbols are added in its anti-clockwise direction.

In all other pairs, the existing figure rotates through 135° clockwise in such a way that first leaf of the figure in clockwise direction gets shaded and the head of the arrow gets reversed. In pair (b), second leaf gets shaded and, hence is different from others.
In all other pairs, the white figure rotates through 90° clockwise and moves two sides clockwise and gets shaded and the black figure is converted into a new figure.

In all other pairs, all the four symbols interchange position in such a way that the symbol at top left position gets inverted and occupies bottom right position, the symbol at bottom right position rotates through 180° and occupies top left position, the symbol at bottom left position rotates through 90° anti-clockwise and occupies top right position and a new symbol appears at bottom left position.
In all other pairs, one arm and one foot of the figure is deleted.

In all other pairs of figures, the first symbol counting in anti-clockwise direction of the blank space moves one step clockwise and is replaced by a new symbol and other two symbols interchange positions.

In all other pairs, the symbol at the top left position gets inverted and occupies the bottom right position, the symbol at the bottom left position rotates through 180° anti-clockwise and occupies the top right position, the symbol at the bottom right position rotates through 180° and occupies the top left position and a new symbol appears at the bottom left position.

In all other pairs of figures, existing figure is changed into a new figure in such a way that the new figure has one side more than the existing figure with total number of lines inside and outside the new figure equal to the number of sides of the existing figure.

The symbol at the top left position rotates through 180° and occupies the bottom right position, the symbol at the top right position gets inverted vertically and occupies the bottom left position, the symbol at the bottom right position rotates through 90° anti-clockwise and occupies the top left position and the figure at the bottom left position occupies the top right position in such a way that the number of sides of the figure is increased by one.

The first, second, third and fourth arrows from the bottom become third, first, second and fourth arrows in such a way that the first arrow gets reversed, the head of the second and third arrows get reversed and the number of sides of the head of fourth arrow is increased by one.
A water image is added on top of the existing figure from section (i) to (ii) in the original pair.

The pin rotates through 180° and moves one side anti-clockwise and a new pin with head joining towards the first pin appears and both the main figures are changed into new figures with one more side each.

From section (i) to section (ii) of the problem figure, the outer semi-circle rotates through 180° or 90° either in clockwise or anti-clockwise direction and the shaded design inside the small circle rotates through 180°.
The symbols appearing in pairs shift to the upper row and appear in singles, whereas the symbols of the upper row shift downwards and appear in pairs.
The pattern of change from (i) to (ii) of the unnumbered pair of figure is as follows: Hence, except figure (b) all other figures follow the same pattern as unnumbered pair of figures.

In the unnumbered pair of figures, from (i) to (ii), the first and last element get reversed at their places while the middle element gets inverted. Hence, except figure (d), all other figures follow the same pattern as unnumbered figure.

The pattern of unnumbered figure is as follows. From (i) to (ii), the first element becomes fourth, second becomes third, third becomes last, fourth becomes first and fifth becomes second from top to bottom. Hence, except figure (a), all other figures follow the same pattern as unnumbered figure.
The whole figure rotates by 90° anti-clockwise. The first and the third and the second and the fourth element interchange positions.
Circles changes to squares and vice-versa from figure (1) to (2).
From figure (1) to (2), the figure rotates by 90° in clockwise direction.
From figure (1) to (2), black circles turns to white diamonds, white circles turn to black diamonds and vice-versa.
From figure (1) to (2), black objects turn to white and vice-versa.
Only the lines that appears three times in the same position in the first three squares are carried forward to the final square.

From figure (3) to (2), the circle goes inside the triangle and the triangle in turn goes inside the square, which turns onto its base. And, from figure (3) to (4), the square goes inside the diamond and the diamond in turn goes inside the pentagon, which turns onto its base.
From figure (1) to (2), square 1 moves to bottom right, square 2 moves to top left, square 3 moves to top right and square 4 moves to bottom left.
From figure (1) to (2), all white segments remain the same but black segments turns white and flip over.
From figure (1) to (2), the shape of string remain as it is but with a reversal of small circles to bigger circles and vice-versa.
From figure (1) to (2), all the figures attached to the central figure remains in the same order and move in one direction.
From figure (1) to (2), only the dots which appear twice in the same segment in the previous three circles are carried forward.
